text:
Siyao Guo
Siyao Guo is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at NYU Shanghai, and a Global Network Associate Professor at the Tandon School of Engineering at NYU. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, she was a Postdoctoral Researcher at New York University, UC Berkeley and Northeastern University. She obtained her PhD from the Chinese University of Hong Kong.
Select Publications
Akshima, Tyler Besselman, Siyao Guo, Zhiye Xie and Yuping Ye: Tight Time-Space Tradeoffs for the Decisional Diffie-Hellman Problem. STOC 2024:1739-1749
Alexander Golovnev, Siyao Guo, Spencer Peters and Noah Stephens-Davidowitz: Revisiting Time-Space Tradeoffs for Function Inversion. CRYPTO (2) 2023: 453-481
Akshima, Siyao Guo, Qipeng Liu: Time-Space Lower Bounds for Finding Collisions in Merkle-Damgård Hash Functions. CRYPTO (3) 2022: 192-221
Nick Gravin, Siyao Guo, Tsz Chiu Kwok, Pinyan Lu: Concentration bounds for almost k-wise independence with applications to non-uniform security. SODA 2021: 2404-2423
Kai-Min Chung, Siyao Guo, Qipeng Liu, Luowen Qian: Tight Quantum Time-Space Tradeoffs for Function Inversion. FOCS 2020: 673-684

text_blocks.text: Siyao Guo is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at NYU Shanghai, and a Global Network Associate Professor at the Tandon School of Engineering at NYU. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, she was a Postdoctoral Researcher at New York University, UC Berkeley and Northeastern University. She obtained her PhD from the Chinese University of Hong Kong.
